training.purpose: Use this page to ask questions about NIF training manuals and get grounded answers based on training resources and key topic summaries.

sections.title: How This Page Starts
points: When you open this page, the app can auto-submit a starter training question to fetch a baseline summary.
The conversation area then shows the user question and the assistant answer.

sections.title: How To Ask Follow-up Questions
points: Type a question in the message box and click Send, or press Enter.
Ask specific follow-ups such as topic scope, coverage gaps, owner teams, or process steps.

sections.title: How To Read The Response
points: Assistant output supports structured formatting such as bullets, emphasis, and links.
Use links in the answer to jump to related references where available.

step.purpose: Use this page to create, continue, and complete a NIF step-by-step session while preserving progress between chats.

sections.title: Session Start Options
points: New NIF chat session starts a fresh guided flow.
Load NIF from previous chat opens saved files so you can resume from prior progress.

sections.title: Responding To Guided Questions
points: You can type answers in the chat box and click Send.
For choice-based questions, use the in-chat option selector cards. Select one or more options when applicable and click Submit option(s).
Use the search box above option cards to quickly find choices in large lists.

sections.title: Action Icons Below Chat
points: Save NIF progress opens a dialog to save the current in-progress NIF file.
Show progress opens a detailed progress grid for the active NIF state.
Reload NIF rules refreshes step-by-step runtime rules from source files.
Download file exports current NIF progress to a CSV file.
Refresh page resets page state and reloads available actions/files.

search.purpose: Use this page to ask natural-language database questions and analyze result sets in the Output Data Explorer grid.

sections.title: Running A Search
points: Enter a business question and click Send.
The assistant returns the answer summary, and the Output Data Explorer shows query rows in tabular form.

sections.title: Output Data Explorer
points: Rows count reflects filtered rows when filters are active.
The grid supports scrolling and pagination for large outputs.

sections.title: Filter Icon
points: Click the filter icon to open advanced filtering.
Choose whether conditions are combined with AND or OR.
Add one or more conditions by selecting a column, operator, and value(s).
Use text, numeric, or date operators based on column type.
Apply saves active filters; Clear removes all filters.

sections.title: Download And SQL Controls
points: Excel download icon exports filtered rows when filters are active; otherwise it exports all visible rows.
Show SQL/Hide SQL toggles the generated SQL statement display.

field.purpose: Sorry! This module is not available at this time

sections.title: How To Use
points: Type a field-related question and click Send.
Ask for definitions, dependencies, data-entry expectations, or examples.

sections.title: Response Guidance
points: Use follow-up questions to narrow by region, process stage, or exception handling.
If output is broad, ask for step-by-step clarification for the specific field.
